sanskrit: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् । रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥
hindiTranslation: विवेकी पुरुषोंने शुभकर्मका तो सात्त्विक निर्मल फल कहा है, राजस कर्मका फल दुःख कहा है और तामस कर्मका फल अज्ञान मूढ़ता कहा है ॥
englishTranslation: The result of wholesome action is pure and clear; the result of rajasic action is suffering; the result of tamasic action is ignorance and delusion.
essence: Action carries its own flavor of result.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Wholesome action leads to a clear, pure result. Restless action leads to suffering. Dark, confused action leads to ignorance and delusion.
simpleInsight: Your actions shape the kind of life you inherit from them.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna explains the three gunas to Arjuna, who is frozen before war. After showing how the gunas affect rebirth, he now teaches how they color the fruits of action itself. The point is to see what kind of result each kind of action creates.
modernRelevance: You send the message in anger, then stare at the screen all night. The words were yours; the fallout was too. This verse helps you see the cost before you act.
translationLiteral: कर्मणः (of action) / सुकृतस्य (of well-done, wholesome) / आहुः (they say) / सात्त्विकं (sattvic, pure) / निर्मलं (stainless, clear) / फलम् (fruit, result) / रजसः (of rajas) / तु (but) / फलम् (fruit, result) / दुःखम् (suffering) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / तमसः (of tamas) / फलम् (fruit, result)
